title: Multivariate mixture clustering with the DPM engine
text: component multivariate normal. conjugate cluster base measure Normal-Inverse-Wishart: Σj∼InvWishart(S0,df0),μj∣Σj∼Nd(μ0,Σj/κ0), \Sigma_j \sim \text{InvWishart}(S_0, df_0), \qquad \mu_j \mid \Sigma_j \sim N_d(\mu_0, \Sigma_j / \kappa_0),  allocation vector following Chinese Restaurant Process. nimix writes NIMBLE’s dmnorm(cov = ...) + dinvwish, pairing NIMBLE recognises conjugate dCRP — efficient collapsed CRP samplers used automatically . defaults data-scaled : mu0 = colMeans(data), prior mean cluster covariance equals cov(data), df0 = d + 2 (validated exceed d + 1 empty-component draws stay non-singular).

title: Multivariate mixture clustering with the DPM engine
text: nimixClust detects Y matrix routes multivariate component (NormalMvSpec). can force family distribution = "normal-mv" ("normal-uv" vector).
code: library(nimix)  set.seed(1) # two well-separated 2-D clusters, K_true = 2 Y <- rbind(  matrix(rnorm(150 * 2, mean = -3), ncol = 2),  matrix(rnorm(150 * 2, mean = 3), ncol = 2) )  fit <- nimixClust(  Y, K_max = 10, distribution = "normal", method = "dpm",  mcmcControl = list(niter = 6000, nburnin = 2000) ) fit

title: Multivariate mixture clustering with the DPM engine
text: univariate case, mixture likelihood invariant permuting component labels, summary relabels first (ECR-ITERATIVE-1), conditioning modal number occupied clusters. multivariate fits component table reports posterior mean mean coordinate (mu_1, mu_2, …) posterior mean marginal variance (var_1, var_2, …); full relabelled mean covariance matrices available fit@relabeled$Sigma_mean.

title: Multivariate mixture clustering with the DPM engine
text: label-switching diagnostics also work multivariate case (plot first mean coordinate across clusters):
code: plot(fit, type = "K") # posterior of the number of occupied clusters plot(fit, type = "cluster") # data coloured by MAP cluster (first two dims) plot(fit, type = "trace_raw") plot(fit, type = "trace_relabeled")

title: Multivariate mixture clustering with the DPM engine
text: multivariate data predictive density evaluated supplied rows rather 1-D grid:
code: newpts <- rbind(c(-3, -3), c(0, 0), c(3, 3)) predict(fit, newdata = newpts)

title: Multivariate mixture clustering with the DPM engine
text: K_max remains truncation level, fixed number clusters. prior covariance scaling matters higher dimensions; data-scaled Normal-Inverse-Wishart default keeps prior weakly informative without becoming vague . small n relative d, identifiability weakens ( ); prefer n comfortably larger d inspect posterior K rather trusting single modal value.

title: Univariate mixture clustering with the DPM engine

title: Mixtures of linear regressions
text: simulate two regression regimes share intercept opposite slopes, unbalanced 80/20 split .
code: library(nimix)  set.seed(1) n <- 250 x <- runif(n, -3, 3) grp <- c(rep(1L, 200), rep(2L, 50)) # 80% / 20% y <- ifelse(grp == 1L, 2 * x, -2 * x) + rnorm(n, 0, 0.7) df <- data.frame(y = y, x = x)

title: Mixtures of linear regressions
text: summary relabels components first (mixture likelihood invariant permuting component labels, raw per-component averages meaningful) reports, per component, mixing weight, regression coefficients named design columns, residual variance.
code: fit <- nimixReg(  y ~ x, data = df,  K_max = 8, method = "dpm",  mcmcControl = list(niter = 4000, nburnin = 1000),  verbose = FALSE ) summary(fit)

title: Mixtures of linear regressions

code: predict(fit, newdata = data.frame(x = c(-2, 0, 2)))  plot(fit, type = "fitted") # observed vs fitted

text: number regimes known assumed, finite-mixture engine fast baseline. uses Dirichlet prior weights categorical allocation, truncation tune.
code: fit2 <- nimixReg(  y ~ x, data = df,  K = 2, method = "fixedk",  mcmcControl = list(niter = 4000, nburnin = 1000),  verbose = FALSE ) summary(fit2)

title: Mixtures of linear regressions
text: Gating constant: mixing weights depend covariates. Covariate-dependent (concomitant) gating planned option. residual-variance prior data-scaled finite prior variance, keeps variance small component collapsing toward zero. clustering, always read component estimates summary (relabels) rather raw MCMC draws.

title: Heavy-tailed components: Student-t vs Normal-Gamma
code: mu <- 1.5; s2 <- 2.3; df <- 5 mix <- function(y) integrate(function(w)   dnorm(y, mu, sqrt(s2 / w)) * dgamma(w, df / 2, df / 2), 0, Inf)$value tdn <- function(y) dt((y - mu) / sqrt(s2), df) / sqrt(s2) ys <- c(-2, 0, 1.5, 4) data.frame(y = ys, scale_mixture = sapply(ys, mix), student_t = sapply(ys, tdn)) # the two columns agree to numerical-integration error

what: Fitting both on the same data
title: Heavy-tailed components: Student-t vs Normal-Gamma
text: recover two clusters. compare sampling efficiency problem, time fit divide effective sample size parameters interest (effective samples per second); Normal-Gamma route usually cheaper per iteration, direct-t route stores latent omega.
code: library(nimix) set.seed(1) y <- c(rt(120, df = 4) - 5, rt(120, df = 4) + 5)   # two heavy-tailed clusters  fit_t <- nimixClust(y, K_max = 8, distribution = "studentt", prior = list(df = 4),                     mcmcControl = list(niter = 4000, nburnin = 1000),                     verbose = FALSE) fit_ng <- nimixClust(y, K_max = 8, distribution = "normalgamma", prior = list(df = 4),                      mcmcControl = list(niter = 4000, nburnin = 1000),                      verbose = FALSE) summary(fit_t) summary(fit_ng)

what: Which to choose – measured, not assumed
title: Heavy-tailed components: Student-t vs Normal-Gamma
text: honest comparison effective samples per second number clusters K (label-invariant quantity). script inst/harness/run_benchmark_heavytail.R runs ; representative numbers one machine (R 4.3.3, NIMBLE 1.4.2, 6000 iterations) : pattern “one route always wins”: clustering, direct Student-t route mixes partition far better (7x ESS per second). Augmenting latent omega couples partition high-dimensional latent vector slows mixing, even omega slice-sampled (van Dyk & Meng 2001). regression, two comparable. conjugate Gibbs update p regression coefficients (Normal-Gamma keeps direct t route gives ) worth , roughly offsetting augmentation penalty. : prefer Student-t clustering; treat two interchangeable regression, choosing Normal-Gamma also want robustness weights omega. Always confirm data benchmark script rather assuming. regression heavy-tailed multivariate errors, note identifiability “pitfalls” small df discussed Fernández & Steel (1999): small df can make quantities weakly identified – property model, bug.

what: Bayesian Mixture Clustering and Regression with NIMBLE
title: Bayesian Mixture Clustering and Regression with NIMBLE
text: nimix Bayesian mixture-modelling package built top NIMBLE. provides mixture clustering (univariate multivariate) mixtures regressions (univariate multivariate response) extensible S4 DistributionSpec contract, new component families plug every inference engine without touching engine code.

title: Bayesian Mixture Clustering and Regression with NIMBLE
text: Clustering — univariate multivariate Gaussian, plus Student-t, Normal-Gamma (heavy-tailed), Poisson Binomial components. Mixture regressions (nimixReg) — Gaussian linear, Student-t Normal-Gamma residuals, Poisson/Binomial GLM components, multivariate (matrix) response via cbind(y1, y2) ~ x. "dpm" — Dirichlet Process Mixture infers number components (NIMBLE’s Chinese Restaurant Process), using K_max. "fixedk" — finite mixture fixed, known K (including single-component K = 1 baseline model comparison). Post-hoc label-switching correction via label.switching package, summary(), plot() predict() methods. reversible-jump MCMC engine (method = "rjmcmc") planned experimental; gated behind validation yet available.

title: Bayesian Mixture Clustering and Regression with NIMBLE
text: nimix depends nimble, compiles model code, need working C/C++ toolchain (Rtools Windows, Xcode CLT macOS, build-essential Linux).

title: Bayesian Mixture Clustering and Regression with NIMBLE
code: library(nimix)  ## Univariate clustering, number of components estimated (DPM) set.seed(1) y <- c(rnorm(100, -3), rnorm(100, 3)) fit <- nimixClust(y, K_max = 8,                   mcmcControl = list(niter = 2000, nburnin = 1000),                   verbose = FALSE) summary(fit) plot(fit, type = "K")  ## Mixture of linear regressions, fixed number of regimes (finite mixture) x  <- runif(200, -3, 3) g  <- rep(1:2, each = 100) df <- data.frame(y = ifelse(g == 1, 2 * x, -2 * x) + rnorm(200, 0, 0.7), x = x) reg <- nimixReg(y ~ x, df, K = 2, method = "fixedk",                 mcmcControl = list(niter = 2000, nburnin = 1000),                 verbose = FALSE) summary(reg)

title: Fitted nimix mixture result — FitResult-class
text: object FitResult. ... Passed relabel relabelling triggered. x FitResult. y Ignored. type One "K" (posterior #clusters), "trace_raw" (raw cluster-parameter traces; zig-zags reveal label switching), "trace_relabeled" (traces relabelling), "density" (univariate clustering : data histogram posterior predictive overlay), "cluster" (multivariate clustering, \(d \ge 2\): scatter coloured MAP cluster), "fitted" (regression : observed response vs posterior predictive mean). newdata Points evaluate: numeric vector (univariate clustering), matrix d columns (multivariate clustering), data frame predictors (regression). Defaults training data. maxDraws Integer cap number posterior draws used (default 500); draws thinned uniformly exceeded.

title: Fitted nimix mixture result — FitResult-class
text: show(FitResult): Compact display fitted result. summary(FitResult): Posterior summary (relabelled component estimates, posterior number clusters, mixing diagnostic). plot(x = FitResult, y = missing): Diagnostic result plots. predict(FitResult): Posterior predictive density new points. Returns posterior predictive density averaged MCMC draws, using occupied-cluster mixture draw (weights = cluster sizes / n). quantity label-invariant, relabelling required. multivariate fits density evaluated supplied rows newdata. regression fit (nimixReg) instead returns posterior predictive mean \(E[y \mid x]\) per row newdata (column .fitted). keep evaluation tractable draws subsampled maxDraws.

title: Fitted nimix mixture result — FitResult-class
text: mcmcSamples matrix monitored MCMC draws (iterations x parameters). Kposterior Integer vector: number occupied clusters per iteration. clusterAllocation Integer matrix (iterations x n) raw cluster indicators xi. paramTrace named list raw cluster-parameter traces (iterations x Kmax matrix). engineUsed Character scalar naming engine. distSpec DistributionSpec used. data data model fit . Kmax Integer truncation level. prior prior list used. relabeled list cache populated relabel (empty). mcmcControl MCMC control list actually used. call matched call.

title: Build the NIMBLE model code for a (distribution, engine) pair — buildModelCode
text: buildModelCode(spec = NormalUvSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Univariate Gaussian DPM model code (dCRP). Builds NIMBLE code $$y_i \sim N(\mu_{\xi_i}, s^2_{\xi_i}), \quad       \xi_{1:n} \sim CRP(\alpha, n),$$ conjugate Normal-Inverse-Gamma cluster prior Gamma hyperprior \(\alpha\). Cluster-parameter vectors length L = K_max (NIMBLE's exact truncation; sampler stays proper long number occupied clusters strictly L). buildModelCode(spec = NormalMvSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Multivariate Gaussian DPM model code (dCRP)   conjugate Normal-Inverse-Wishart cluster base measure. Builds NIMBLE code $$y_i \sim N_d(\mu_{\xi_i}, \Sigma_{\xi_i}), \quad       \xi_{1:n} \sim CRP(\alpha, n),$$ \(\Sigma_j \sim InvWishart(S_0, df_0)\) \(\mu_j \sim N_d(\mu_0, \Sigma_j / \kappa_0)\). dimension \(d\) constant index ranges 1:d expand model-build time. buildModelCode(spec = NormalRegSpec, engine = DPMEngine): DPM mixture--linear-regressions model code   (dCRP) conjugate Normal-Inverse-Gamma cluster prior constant   (CRP) gating. Builds NIMBLE code $$y_i \sim N(x_i^\top \beta_{\xi_i}, s^2_{\xi_i}), \quad       \xi_{1:n} \sim CRP(\alpha, n),$$ \(s^2_j \sim InvGamma(nu_0, s_0)\) \(\beta_j \mid s^2_j \sim N_p(b_0, s^2_j B_0)\). number predictors \(p\) design matrix \(X\) constants. buildModelCode(spec = PoissonRegSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Poisson GLM regression DPM code (log link). buildModelCode(spec = PoissonRegSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Poisson GLM regression fixed-K code (log link). buildModelCode(spec = BinomialRegSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Binomial GLM regression DPM code (logit link). buildModelCode(spec = BinomialRegSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Binomial GLM regression fixed-K code (logit link). buildModelCode(spec = StudentTRegSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Student-t regression DPM code (direct t density; scale enters precision tau = 1 / s2). buildModelCode(spec = StudentTRegSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Student-t regression fixed-K code. buildModelCode(spec = NormalGammaRegSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Normal-Gamma regression DPM code (scale mixture; conjugate coefficients conditional omega). buildModelCode(spec = NormalGammaRegSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Normal-Gamma regression fixed-K code. buildModelCode(spec = NormalMvRegSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Multivariate-response Normal regression DPM code. buildModelCode(spec = NormalMvRegSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Multivariate-response Normal regression fixed-K. buildModelCode(spec = StudentTMvRegSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Multivariate-response Student-t regression DPM code (direct multivariate-t kernel). buildModelCode(spec = StudentTMvRegSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Multivariate-response Student-t regression fixed-K. buildModelCode(spec = NormalGammaMvRegSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Multivariate-response Normal-Gamma regression DPM code (scale mixture; conjugate cluster updates). buildModelCode(spec = NormalGammaMvRegSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Multivariate-response Normal-Gamma regression fixed-K code. buildModelCode(spec = StudentTUvSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Univariate Student-t DPM model code (dCRP). t density evaluated directly; df constant. buildModelCode(spec = NormalGammaUvSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Normal-Gamma scale-mixture DPM model code (dCRP) per-observation latent precision multiplier omega. buildModelCode(spec = StudentTMvSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Multivariate Student-t DPM code using user-defined dmvt_nimix kernel Normal-Inverse-Wishart cluster prior. buildModelCode(spec = NormalGammaMvSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Multivariate Normal-Gamma scale-mixture DPM code per-observation latent precision multiplier omega. buildModelCode(spec = PoissonSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Poisson DPM model code (Gamma-Poisson conjugate). buildModelCode(spec = BinomialSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Binomial DPM model code (Beta-Binomial conjugate). buildModelCode(spec = NormalUvSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Univariate Gaussian finite-mixture code (fixed K). buildModelCode(spec = NormalMvSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Multivariate Gaussian finite-mixture code (fixed K). buildModelCode(spec = NormalRegSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Mixture--linear-regressions finite-mixture code (fixed K). buildModelCode(spec = StudentTUvSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Univariate Student-t finite-mixture code (fixed K). buildModelCode(spec = NormalGammaUvSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Univariate Normal-Gamma (scale-mixture Student-t) finite-mixture code (fixed K). buildModelCode(spec = NormalGammaMvSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Multivariate Normal-Gamma scale-mixture finite-mixture code (fixed K). buildModelCode(spec = StudentTMvSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Multivariate Student-t finite-mixture code (fixed K) using user-defined dmvt_nimix kernel. buildModelCode(spec = PoissonSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Poisson finite-mixture code (fixed K). buildModelCode(spec = BinomialSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Binomial finite-mixture code (fixed K).

title: Build a data-scaled default prior for a distribution — defaultPrior
text: defaultPrior(NormalUvSpec): Data-scaled Normal-Inverse-Gamma prior. Control overrides: cLoc (location spread multiplier; prior sd mu ~ cLoc * sd(data), default 2), nu0 (InvGamma shape, default 3, must exceed 2 finite prior variance), concPrior. defaultPrior(NormalMvSpec): Data-scaled Normal-Inverse-Wishart prior (multivariate). Control overrides: cLoc (mean-dispersion multiplier; prior covariance mu Sigma / kappa0 kappa0 = 1 / cLoc^2, default cLoc = 2) df0 (inverse-Wishart degrees freedom, default d + 2, must exceed d + 1 finite, non-singular prior covariance empty components). defaultPrior(NormalRegSpec): Data-scaled Normal-Inverse-Gamma g-prior   regression component. Requires design matrix control$X. Control overrides: g (g-prior factor; prior covariance beta s2 * g * solve(crossprod(X)), default g = n, unit-information prior) nu0 (InvGamma shape, default 3, must exceed 2 finite prior variance). defaultPrior(PoissonRegSpec): g-prior coefficients. defaultPrior(BinomialRegSpec): g-prior coefficients; needs size. defaultPrior(StudentTRegSpec): NIG g-prior plus fixed df (default 4, > 2). defaultPrior(NormalGammaRegSpec): NIG g-prior plus fixed df (default 4, > 2). defaultPrior(NormalMvRegSpec): Inverse-Wishart Sigma + matrix-normal coefficients. defaultPrior(StudentTMvRegSpec): Inverse-Wishart + matrix-normal plus fixed df. defaultPrior(NormalGammaMvRegSpec): Inverse-Wishart + matrix-normal plus fixed df. defaultPrior(StudentTUvSpec): Data-scaled Normal location / Gamma precision prior   Student-t component. Control overrides: cLoc (location spread multiplier, default 2), df (degrees freedom, fixed hyperparameter, default 4, must exceed 2 finite component variance). defaultPrior(NormalGammaUvSpec): Normal-Inverse-Gamma prior plus fixed df (degrees freedom, default 4, must exceed 2 finite component variance). defaultPrior(StudentTMvSpec): Normal-Inverse-Wishart prior plus fixed df (default 5, must exceed 2 finite component covariance). defaultPrior(NormalGammaMvSpec): Normal-Inverse-Wishart prior plus fixed df (default 5, must exceed 2 finite component covariance). defaultPrior(PoissonSpec): Data-scaled Gamma prior Poisson rate (E[lambda] ~= mean(y)). defaultPrior(BinomialSpec): Data-scaled Beta prior success probability. Requires number trials control$size.

title: Bayesian mixture clustering — nimixClust
text: Fit Bayesian Gaussian mixture model clustering. Two engines available: finite mixture fixed, known number components (method = "fixedk", using argument K) Dirichlet Process Mixture infers number components (method = "dpm", using truncation level K_max). component family (univariate multivariate Gaussian) chosen shape data distribution argument.

title: Bayesian mixture clustering — nimixClust
text: data numeric vector (univariate) numeric matrix one row per observation one column per dimension (multivariate). single-column matrix treated univariate. K Integer number components finite mixture (method = "fixedk"). Required method; must given method = "dpm" (use K_max ). K_max Integer truncation level Dirichlet Process Mixture (method = "dpm"); number components estimated bound. data-aware default min(10, floor(n / 5)) used missing. Must given method = "fixedk" (use K). distribution Component distribution. "normal" (default) picks univariate multivariate Gaussian automatically data shape; "normal-uv" / "normal-mv" force specific one. Student-t / Poisson / Binomial planned v0.4.0. method Engine: "dpm" (default; estimate number components), "fixedk" (finite mixture known K), "rjmcmc" (planned v0.5.0, currently errors). prior named list prior overrides passed defaultPrior (univariate: cLoc, nu0; multivariate: cLoc, df0) plus, DPM, optional concPrior = c(shape, rate) concentration, , finite mixture, dirichletConc Dirichlet weight prior. mcmcControl named list niter, nburnin, thin. initMethod Initialisation cluster allocation: "kmeans" (default, dispersed start) "single". seed Integer RNG seed reproducibility. verbose Logical; print NIMBLE configuration progress. FALSE, NIMBLE's compilation notes silenced.

title: Bayesian mixture clustering — nimixClust
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(1)  ## Univariate, number of clusters estimated (DPM) y <- c(rnorm(100, -3, 1), rnorm(100, 3, 1)) fit <- nimixClust(y, K_max = 8,                   mcmcControl = list(niter = 2000, nburnin = 1000),                   verbose = FALSE) summary(fit) #> Relabelling MCMC output before summarising (label switching)... #> nimix mixture summary (engine: dpm, distribution: normal-uv) #> Observations: 200 (dimension d = 1) #> Relabelling: ECR-ITERATIVE-1 conditioned on modal K = 2 (701 draws) #>  #> Posterior of number of occupied clusters: #>  #>     2     3     4     5  #> 0.701 0.252 0.042 0.005  #>  #> Relabelled component estimates (posterior mean; CIs for univariate): #>  component weight mu_mean mu_lwr mu_upr s2_mean s2_lwr s2_upr #>          1  0.499   -2.89  -3.09  -2.69    1.16  0.886   1.50 #>          2  0.501    2.95   2.73   3.17    1.27  0.939   1.74 #>  #> Mixing diagnostic (single chain): ESS(alpha) = 679, ESS(#clusters) = 303 #> Note: cross-chain Rhat requires multiple chains (planned v0.9.0). plot(fit, type = "K")   ## Univariate, fixed number of components (finite mixture) fit2 <- nimixClust(y, K = 2, method = "fixedk",                    mcmcControl = list(niter = 2000, nburnin = 1000),                    verbose = FALSE) summary(fit2) #> Relabelling MCMC output before summarising (label switching)... #> Warning: Low effective sample size for the cluster count (ESS = 0 of 1000 draws): the chain may be mixing poorly across partitions. Consider a longer run or k-means initialisation. #> nimix mixture summary (engine: fixedk, distribution: normal-uv) #> Observations: 200 (dimension d = 1) #> Relabelling: ECR-ITERATIVE-1 conditioned on modal K = 2 (1000 draws) #>  #> Posterior of number of occupied clusters: #>  #> 2  #> 1  #>  #> Relabelled component estimates (posterior mean; CIs for univariate): #>  component weight mu_mean mu_lwr mu_upr s2_mean s2_lwr s2_upr #>          1  0.499   -2.89  -3.10  -2.68    1.15  0.863   1.53 #>          2  0.501    2.95   2.73   3.18    1.28  0.958   1.69 #>  #> Mixing diagnostic (single chain): ESS(alpha) = NA, ESS(#clusters) = 0 #> Note: cross-chain Rhat requires multiple chains (planned v0.9.0).  ## Multivariate (2-D), DPM Y <- rbind(matrix(rnorm(200, -2), ncol = 2),            matrix(rnorm(200,  2), ncol = 2)) fitMv <- nimixClust(Y, K_max = 8,                     mcmcControl = list(niter = 2000, nburnin = 1000),                     verbose = FALSE) summary(fitMv) #> Relabelling MCMC output before summarising (label switching)... #> nimix mixture summary (engine: dpm, distribution: normal-mv) #> Observations: 200 (dimension d = 2) #> Relabelling: ECR-ITERATIVE-1 conditioned on modal K = 3 (345 draws) #>  #> Posterior of number of occupied clusters: #>  #>     2     3     4     5     6     7     8  #> 0.086 0.345 0.283 0.180 0.068 0.034 0.004  #>  #> Relabelled component estimates (posterior mean; CIs for univariate): #>  component weight   mu_1    mu_2 var_1 var_2 #>          1  0.430  2.051  2.0914  1.05 0.882 #>          2  0.466 -2.272 -2.1278  1.01 1.068 #>          3  0.104  0.603  0.0246  1.79 1.380 #>  #> Mixing diagnostic (single chain): ESS(alpha) = 250, ESS(#clusters) = 81 #> Note: cross-chain Rhat requires multiple chains (planned v0.9.0). plot(fitMv, type = "cluster")  # }

title: nimix 0.4.3
text: Bug-fix packaging/infrastructure release. new modelling features; clears R CMD check adds continuous integration documentation site ahead v0.5.x reversible-jump work.

title: nimix 0.4.3
text: Namespace imports R_CHECK_DEPENDS_ONLY. Added roxygen @import / @importFrom tags (notably @import methods) package doc regenerated NAMESPACE keeps import directives. Previously document()-regenerated NAMESPACE dropped , .onLoad()’s new("NormalUvSpec") failed find function “new” package loaded stated dependencies. Poisson / Binomial regression recovery. Replaced global-GLM initialisation (one pooled fit copied every cluster, k-means raw response) dispersed-slope start plus one hard classification step. Components differ sign slope (crossing regression lines) now separated initialisation, finite-mixture sampler recovers per-component slopes. Single-component finite mixture (K = 1). nimixReg(..., K = 1, method = "fixedk") (clustering analogue) failed build inconsistent dimensionality provided node ‘alphaVec’: length-1 Dirichlet parameter demoted scalar. weight-vector dimensions now declared explicitly, K = 1 baseline (useful WAIC model comparison) builds. Count-response validation. nimixReg() / nimixClust() now reject continuous response distribution = "poisson" / "binomial" early clear message instead surfacing numerical underflow inside sampler.

title: nimix 0.4.3
text: Added GitHub Actions workflows R CMD check (multi-OS) pkgdown documentation site, _pkgdown.yml reference layout, corresponding .Rbuildignore entries.

title: nimix 0.4.2
text: Multivariate-response mixture regression.

title: nimix 0.4.2
text: NormalMvRegSpec: y ~ N_d(X B_k, Sigma_k). StudentTMvRegSpec: direct multivariate-t errors via user-defined dmvt_nimix density (non-conjugate). NormalGammaMvRegSpec: conjugate scale-mixture (omega ~ Gamma(df/2, df/2), y ~ N_d(X B_k, Sigma_k / omega)), omega slice-sampled. Cluster prior: conjugate matrix-Normal-Inverse-Wishart Backlund & Hobert (2020) – inverse-Wishart error covariance , coefficient row, prior covariance v0[l] * Sigma g-prior among-row scale v0 = g * diag((X'X)^{-1}). Conditional Sigma coefficients Gaussian, collapsed CRP cluster sampler kept. predict() returns one fitted column per response. Verified: three recover component coefficient matrices DPM fixed-K; dmvt_nimix kernel scale mixture run inside CRP dynamically indexed coefficient matrices.

title: nimix 0.4.2
text: inst/harness/run_benchmark_heavytail.R normal-gamma-vs-studentt vignette now report measured effective-sample-size-per-second. direct Student-t route mixes partition far better clustering (7x), regression two routes comparable (conjugate coefficient update offsets augmentation penalty) – choose measurement, assumption.

title: nimix 0.4.2
text: Zellner (1976); Fernandez & Steel (1999); Backlund & Hobert (2020) multivariate regression heavy-tailed / scale-mixture errors.

title: nimix 0.4.1
text: Heavy-tailed mixture regression regression-routing fix.

title: nimix 0.4.1
text: StudentTRegSpec: direct t kernel y ~ t(X beta_k, tau_k, df) (non-conjugate). NormalGammaRegSpec: conjugate scale mixture omega ~ Gamma(df/2, df/2), y ~ N(X beta_k, s2_k / omega) – pure Gibbs sampler keeping conjugate NIG g-prior coefficients (Geweke 1993), omega slice-sampled interpretable robustness weights. inherit g-prior trace/relabel handling NormalRegSpec; df fixed hyperparameter (> 2). Verified: recover two regression regimes DPM fixed-K heavy-tailed, outlier-contaminated data.

title: nimix 0.4.1
text: isRegressionSpec() now returns TRUE NormalRegSpec, predict() plot() route Gaussian linear regression regression branch (regression introduced 0.4.0 via GLM hooks; restores routing Normal-linear case).

title: nimix 0.4.1
text: Multivariate-response heavy-tail regression (multivariate-t / Normal-Gamma errors, matrix coefficients) theoretically supported (Zellner 1976; Fernandez & Steel 1999; Backlund & Hobert 2020) working prototype recovers matrix coefficients CRP. needs matrix-response RegressionMixModel (real architecture step) proposed v0.4.2 rather bundled , keep verification rigorous.

title: nimix 0.4.0
text: Fourth development release. Roadmap scope: finalise public registerDistribution(), add Student-t Normal-Gamma (univariate multivariate) plus Poisson Binomial components – existing DPM fixed-K engines, adding DistributionSpec subclasses (engine changes), extensibility claim made concrete.

what: Added – heavy-tail components (a matched pair, identical marginal)
title: nimix 0.4.0
text: StudentTUvSpec / StudentTMvSpec: Student-t components evaluated direct t density. NIMBLE multivariate-t, multivariate kernel supplied user-defined NIMBLE distribution (dmvt_nimix, registered load) – mechanism registerDistribution() offers users. Non-conjugate cluster updates. NormalGammaUvSpec / NormalGammaMvSpec: conjugate scale-mixture route Student-t / multivariate-t marginal, via per-observation latent precision multiplier omega (y ~ N(mu, Sigma/omega), omega ~ Gamma(df/2, df/2)). Conditional omega kernel Gaussian, conjugate Normal-Inverse-Gamma / Normal-Inverse-Wishart cluster updates kept (cheaper per iteration direct t density). latent omega double robustness weights. inherit NormalUvSpec / NormalMvSpec. marginal equivalence (scale-mixture == analytic t / multivariate-t) verified numerically ~1e-10, univariate multivariate. df fixed hyperparameter (default 4 univariate, 5 multivariate; must exceed 2). marginal reached two ways, offered distinct choices different sampling cost – different distributions, unrelated Normal-Gamma shrinkage prior.

title: nimix 0.4.0
text: PoissonSpec (Gamma-Poisson conjugate) BinomialSpec (Beta-Binomial conjugate; number trials size supplied prior). show DistributionSpec contract tied continuous data.

title: nimix 0.4.0
text: distribution = "studentt" / "normalgamma" auto-selects univariate multivariate variant data shape ("normal" already ); "poisson" / "binomial" select count components. work method = "dpm" method = "fixedk". registerDistribution() (public) registers user DistributionSpec objects; dmvt_nimix demonstrates pairing custom spec custom NIMBLE density.

title: nimix 0.4.0
text: nimixReg() now accepts distribution = "poisson" (log link) "binomial" (logit link, trials prior = list(size = ...)), addition "normal" (identity link). component GLM coefficient vector: Poisson y ~ Poisson(exp(X beta_k)), Binomial y ~ Binomial(size, plogis(X beta_k)). “extend GLM” item regression module. New PoissonRegSpec / BinomialRegSpec (g-prior coefficients). GLM likelihood non-conjugate, cluster coefficients updated non-conjugately (Polya-Gamma augmentation, keeping dependencies unchanged avoiding augmentation mixing penalty). Verified: recover two regression regimes DPM fixed-K; predict() applies inverse link. New polymorphic hooks isRegressionSpec() linkInv() route predict path apply component link without class-name branching.

title: nimix 0.4.0
text: latent precision multipliers omega now slice-sampled (via new polymorphic customizeSamplers() hook), random-walk sampled. controlled run raised effective sample size number clusters ~41 ~314 (7x). Even , empirical comparison found direct Student-t route mixes partition markedly better Normal-Gamma route equal lower cost; Normal-Gamma preferred per-observation robustness weights omega wanted, speed. corrects earlier expectation; see knowledge patch.

title: nimix 0.4.0
text: Student-t / Normal-Gamma, univariate multivariate, recover two heavy-tailed clusters DPM fixed-K; custom dmvt_nimix kernel compiles runs inside CRP model. Poisson (rates 2/12) Binomial (probs 0.2/0.75, size 20) recover components.

what: Deferred (still planned for the 0.4.x line / later)
title: nimix 0.4.0
text: Heavy-tail residuals nimixReg() (regression t / Normal-Gamma errors) benchmark vignette comparing two routes remaining 0.4.x items. RJMCMC remains v0.5.0+ (experimental, conditional).

title: nimix 0.3.0
text: Third development release. Scope follows official roadmap (project knowledge, Section 4): v0.3.0 = RegressionMixModel + nimixReg(), DPM mixture linear regressions.

title: nimix 0.3.0
text: FixedKEngine — finite mixture fixed, known number components (method = "fixedk", argument K). Planned since start roadmap simplest engine; now implemented. Uses symmetric Dirichlet prior mixing weights categorical allocation (Chinese Restaurant Process), NIMBLE assigns conjugate samplers weights component parameters DPM truncation reminder arise. Works univariate, multivariate, regression components. Useful fast baseline K known, classical model selection comparing fits across values K. nimixClust() / nimixReg() gained argument K (method = "fixedk") alongside K_max (method = "dpm"), validation errors clearly two swapped. nimixReg(formula, data, ...): Bayesian Dirichlet Process Mixture Gaussian linear regressions. component ; number components inferred via Chinese Restaurant Process (fixed method = "fixedk"). Gating constant (mixing weights depend covariates); covariate-dependent (concomitant) gating planned opt-currently errors pointer. NormalRegSpec: Normal-linear regression component conjugate Normal-Inverse-Gamma g-prior (beta | s2 ~ N(b0, s2 * g * (X'X)^{-1}), unit-information g = n; s2 ~ InvGamma(nu0, s0) data-scaled global OLS residual variance, nu0 >= 3 residual variance collapse small components). Verified: NIMBLE assigns conjugate collapsed CRP samplers (CRP_cluster_wrapper) betaTilde s2Tilde. RegressionMixModel (extends MixtureModel) carrying formula design matrix X. summary() reports relabelled per-component regression coefficients (named design columns) residual variances; predict(fit, newdata) returns posterior predictive mean ; plot(fit, type = "fitted") shows observed vs fitted.

title: nimix 0.3.0
text: Engines now selected polymorphically runEngine generic dispatches engine (DPMEngine vs FixedKEngine); shared helper build / compile / run / extract, two engines share one code path. Component initialisation engine-agnostic (componentInits), returning allocation plus component-parameter starts engines reuse. verbose = FALSE, NIMBLE’s compilation notes CRP truncation reminder silenced, quiet runs examples produce clean output. Code comments roxygen longer cite internal design-document section numbers; explanatory content kept inline. deterministic-node patterns established multivariate model reused regression: s2-scaled coefficient covariance bound node covBeta, dynamically indexed cluster coefficients resolved via betaObs.

title: nimix 0.3.0
text: Covariate-dependent (concomitant) gating — planned opt-(Section 9.8). Student-t / Normal-Gamma / Poisson / Binomial components v0.4.0. RJMCMC engine v0.5.0+ (experimental, conditional — Section 4.0).

title: nimix 0.3.0
text: Pure-R unit tests (g-prior scaling, NIG invariants, simulateParams) end--end unbalanced 80/20 recovery (two regression regimes slopes +2 / -2) pass R 4.3.3 NIMBLE 1.4.2: modal K = 2, recovered slopes 2.00 / -1.93 weights 0.78 / 0.22. full CRAN harness still run release considered CRAN-ready (Section 8, 0.3.4).

title: nimix 0.2.0
text: Second development release. Scope follows official roadmap (project knowledge, Section 4): v0.2.0 = NormalMvSpec (multivariate Gaussian) + multivariate nimixClust() DPM (dCRP) engine + label switching.

title: nimix 0.2.0
text: NormalMvSpec: multivariate Gaussian component conjugate Normal-Inverse-Wishart cluster base measure (dmnorm(cov=) + dinvwish), data-scaled default (mu0 = colMeans, prior mean cluster covariance = cov(data), mean dispersion cLoc-scaled). inverse-Wishart degrees freedom validated exceed d + 1 prior draws empty components finite non-singular (project knowledge Section 9.3). nimixClust() now accepts numeric matrix (one row per observation) routes NormalMvSpec; numeric vector still routes NormalUvSpec. distribution = "normal" chooses univariate/multivariate data shape; "normal-uv" / "normal-mv" force specific family. Dimension-agnostic DPM engine: engine now delegates dimension-specific work (constants, data, inits, trace parsing, relabelled summaries) new DistributionSpec generics — buildConstants(), buildDataList(), dpmInits(), extractParamTraces(), relabelComponents(). Adding distribution longer touches engine (project knowledge Section 3, extensibility seam). relabel() generalised: label-permutation derivation (wrapping label.switching ECR) depends allocations, identical univariate multivariate; parameter permutation component summaries delegated spec. plot() gains type = "cluster" (multivariate : scatter first two dimensions coloured MAP cluster); summary()/predict() dimension-aware (multivariate predictive density via spec’s componentDensity(), draw subsampling tractability). Multivariate recovery scenario added inst/harness/run_recovery_suite.R tests/testthat/test-dist-normal-mv.R (verified recover two well-separated 2-D clusters).

title: nimix 0.2.0
text: nimble moved Imports Depends. NIMBLE’s BUGS-language distributions (dCRP, dinvwish, …) resolvable model building nimble attached, merely imported (convention used NIMBLE-extension packages). fixes latent model-build error (R function 'dCRP' ... exist). Multivariate parameterisation note: conjugate base measure written cov= + dinvwish rather prec= + dwish. two mathematically equivalent, precision/dwish path triggers Cholesky-lifting failure dynamic CRP indexing NIMBLE 1.4.x, whereas cov= + dinvwish compiles, runs, assigned NIMBLE’s conjugate collapsed CRP samplers (verified empirically). ClusterModel data slot widened accept matrices preserving dimensions; univariate behaviour unchanged. univariate engine/relabel logic relocated NormalUvSpec methods (behavioural change; required dimension-agnostic engine).

title: nimix 0.2.0
text: nimixReg() (mixture--regressions) v0.3.0 (stub still errors). Student-t / Normal-Gamma / Poisson / Binomial v0.4.0. RJMCMC engine v0.5.0+ (experimental, conditional — project knowledge Section 4.0).

title: nimix 0.2.0
text: Pure-R unit tests (spec routing, data-scaled prior, NIW invariants, multivariate-normal density, simulateParams) end--end multivariate DPM recovery (two 2-D clusters) pass R 4.3.3 NIMBLE 1.4.2. full CRAN harness (R CMD check ---cran, multi-seed recovery suite, vignette build) still run release considered CRAN-ready (project knowledge Section 8, 0.3.4).

title: nimix 0.1.0
text: First development release. Scope follows official roadmap (project knowledge, Section 4): v0.1.0 = S4 foundation + NormalUvSpec + univariate nimixClust() DPM (dCRP) engine.

title: nimix 0.1.0
text: DistributionSpec (virtual) generics validateParams(), defaultPrior(), buildModelCode(). NormalUvSpec: univariate Gaussian component conjugate Normal-Inverse-Gamma cluster prior (data-scaled default; see project knowledge Section 9.2). MixtureModel (virtual) ClusterModel. EngineConfig (virtual) DPMEngine wrapping NIMBLE’s native dCRP / CRP samplers (Neal 2000). FitResult summary(), plot(), predict(), relabel(). nimixClust() user-facing function (univariate, method = "dpm"). relabel() wraps label.switching (ECR-ITERATIVE-1 default, Papastamoulis & Iliopoulos 2010), conditioning modal number occupied clusters. registerDistribution() / getDistribution() registry skeleton. Recovery-test harness (inst/harness/run_recovery_suite.R) CRAN check harness (inst/harness/run_cran_check.R).

title: nimix 0.1.0
text: nimixReg() (mixture--regressions) v0.3.0. stub exported errors informative message rather silently reordering roadmap (project knowledge Section 0.3.1). Multivariate clustering (NormalMvSpec) v0.2.0. RJMCMC engine v0.5.0+.

title: nimix 0.1.0
text: Package user-facing functions consistently named nimix (nimixClust(), nimixReg()), matching interface signatures project-knowledge document (Section 3). earlier draft used mixed-case spelling nimMix*; renamed throughout consistency, package/document naming discrepancy remains.
